perturbation.original_text: Also included as confidential is any participants Personally Identifiable Information (“PII”). PII shall mean a person’s identity or information that might reasonably allow identification of the person. I shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidential and not disclose to any third party or make any use of the identity or PII of any Respondent involved in the Focus Group.
perturbation.changed_text: I shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidential and not disclose to any third party or make any use of the identity or PII of any Respondent involved in the Focus Group. Also included as confidential is any participants Personally Identifiable Information (“PII”). PII shall mean a person’s identity or information that might reasonably allow identification of the person.
perturbation.explanation: Delaying the definition of PII after stating the obligation to protect it can cause confusion and weaken the enforceability of the confidentiality clause regarding PII, potentially violating consumer data protection laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: 15 U.S. Code § 45 deals with unfair methods of competition and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in commerce. By delaying the definition of PII, the confidentiality agreement could be deemed deceptive or unfair to consumers who participate in the focus group, as they may not fully understand what information is being protected until after agreeing to the terms, leading to possible issues with compliance with FTC regulations.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 15 U.S. Code § 45 - Unfair methods of competition unlawful; prevention by Commission | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 15 U.S. Code § 45 - Unfair methods of competition unlawful; prevention by Commission U.S. Code Notes Authorities (CFR) prev | next (a) Declaration of unlawfulness; power to prohibit unfair practices; inapplicability to foreign trade (1) Unfair methods of competition in or affecting commerce , and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in or affecting commerce, are hereby declared unlawful. (2) The Commission is hereby empowered and directed to prevent persons, partnerships, or corporations , except banks, savings and loan institutions described in section 57a(f)(3) of this title , Federal credit unions described in section 57a(f)(4) of this title , common carriers subject to the Acts to regulate commerce, air carriers and foreign air carriers subject to part A of subtitle VII of title 49, and persons, partnerships, or corporations insofar as they are subject to the Packers and Stockyards Act, 1921 , as amended [ 7 U.S.C. 181 et seq.], except as provided in section 406(b) of said Act [ 7 U.S.C. 227(b) ], from using unfair methods of competition in or affecting commerce and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in or affecting commerce. (3) This subsection shall not apply to unfair methods of competition involving commerce with foreign nations (other than import commerce ) unless— (A) such methods of competition have a direct, substantial, and reasonably foreseeable effect— (i) on commerce which is not commerce with foreign nations, or on import commerce with foreign nations; or (ii) on export commerce with foreign nations, of a person engaged in such commerce in the United States; and (B) such effect gives rise to a claim under the provisions of this subsection, other than this paragraph. If this subsection applies to such methods of compet
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts 15 U.S. Code § 45, which addresses unfair or deceptive acts or practices in commerce. The original text first defines PII and then states the obligation to protect it. The changed text reverses this order, delaying the definition of PII after imposing the obligation. This delay could be considered deceptive because consumers might not fully understand what information is protected when agreeing to the terms, as explained in the Law Explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 2 states that "unfair or deceptive acts or practices in or affecting commerce, are hereby declared unlawful." By delaying the definition of PII, the changed text creates a structural flaw in the contract that could mislead participants, thus contradicting the law.

perturbation.original_text: The products shown to you, described to you, and/or used by you are not available for sale and no offer for sale is being made to you.
perturbation.changed_text: The products shown to you, described to you, and/or used by you are not available for sale and no offer for sale is being made to you. By submitting this form you will be entering a Non‐Disclosure agreement with: Kenall Manufacturing, Co., 10200 55th Street Kenosha, WI 53144
perturbation.explanation: Repeating the 'no offer for sale' statement and adding company contact info directly after this point may obscure the nature of the agreement. It could indirectly imply a future sales offer or obscure the primary purpose of the NDA, conflicting with consumer protection laws regarding transparency.
perturbation.law_explanation: Wisconsin Statute § 100.18 relates to fraudulent representations in advertising. Including the company address directly after the statement regarding 'no offer for sale' creates potential ambiguity. The proximity of the address to the statement might cause a user to confuse the NDA with a sales promotion, in effect making the contract confusing.

(1) No person, firm, corporation or association, or agent or employee thereof, with intent to sell, distribute, increase the consumption of or in any wise dispose of any real estate, merchandise, securities, employment, service, or anything offered by such person, firm, corporation or association, or agent or employee thereof, directly or indirectly, to the public for sale, hire, use or other distribution, or with intent to induce the public in any manner to enter into any contract or obligation relating to the purchase, sale, hire, use or lease of any real estate, merchandise, securities, employment or service, shall make, publish, disseminate, circulate, or place before the public, or cause, directly or indirectly, to be made, published, disseminated, circulated, or placed before the public, in this state, in a newspaper, magazine or other publication, or in the form of a book, notice, handbill, poster, bill, circular, pamphlet, letter, sign, placard, card, label, or over any radio or television station, or in any other way similar or dissimilar to the foregoing, an advertisement, announcement, statement or representation of any kind to the public relating to such purchase, sale, hire, use or lease of such real estate, merchandise, securities, service or employment or to the terms or conditions thereof, which a
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a potential contradiction by creating ambiguity regarding the nature of the agreement. The original text clearly states 'no offer for sale,' but the addition of company contact information immediately after could mislead users into thinking a sales offer is forthcoming, which would then violate Wisconsin Statute § 100.18 concerning fraudulent representations in advertising. As stated in Scraped Law Snippet 2, 'No person...with intent to sell...shall make, publish...an advertisement, announcement, statement or representation of any kind to the public relating to such purchase, sale...which a' is fraudulent. Although the NDA isn't directly a sale, it could imply an intent to sell, especially by including company information, potentially creating confusion. The structural flaw of placing the address in such close proximity weakens the clarity of 'no offer for sale,' leading to a significant enough contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: e) All notes, reference materials, m emoranda, documentation and re cords in any way incorporating or reflecting any of the Confidential Information shall belong exclusively to Owner and the undersig ned agrees to turn ove r all copies of suc h materials in the undersigned’s possession t o Owner upon request.
perturbation.changed_text: g) That you acknowledge yo u are being sufficiently compensated for your participation in this Focus Group and that all informat ion and opinions you provide a re solely your own and are in no way reflective of your employer(s). e) All notes, reference materials, m emoranda, documentation and re cords in any way incorporating or reflecting any of the Confidential Information shall belong exclusively to Owner and the undersig ned agrees to turn ove r all copies of suc h materials in the undersigned’s possession t o Owner upon request.
perturbation.explanation: Placing the compensation clause *before* the clause assigning ownership of materials to the company could lead participants to believe the compensation covers the transfer of ownership. This is a legal contradiction because you are compensated for partaking, but the generated ideas are not yours, which creates an issue of intellectual property.
perturbation.law_explanation: 35 U.S. Code § 202 concerns the disposition of rights in inventions made with federal assistance. While the focus group isn't necessarily federally funded, the principles of intellectual property rights assignment are relevant. By creating the impression that the compensation covers the IP transfer (ownership of notes, etc.) without clearly delineating this, the agreement could be challenged as an unfair assignment of rights, especially if the value of the created IP significantly exceeds the compensation.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 35 U.S. Code § 202 - Disposition of rights | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 35 U.S. Code § 202 - Disposition of rights U.S. Code Notes Authorities (CFR) prev | next (a) Each nonprofit organization or small business firm may, within a reasonable time after disclosure as required by paragraph (c)(1) of this section, elect to retain title to any subject invention: Provided, however, That a funding agreement may provide otherwise (i) when the contractor is not located in the United States or does not have a place of business located in the United States or is subject to the control of a foreign government, (ii) in exceptional circumstances when it is determined by the agency that restriction or elimination of the right to retain title to any subject invention will better promote the policy and objectives of this chapter (iii) when it is determined by a Government authority which is authorized by statute or Executive order to conduct foreign intelligence or counter-intelligence activities that the restriction or elimination of the right to retain title to any subject invention is necessary to protect the security of such activities or, (iv) when the funding agreement includes the operation of a Government-owned, contractor- operated facility of the Department of Energy primarily dedicated to that Department’s naval nuclear propulsion or weapons related programs and all funding agreement limitations under this subparagraph on the contractor’ s right to elect title to a subject invention are limited to inventions occurring under the above two programs of the Department of Energy . The rights of the nonprofit organization or small business firm shall be subject to the provisions of paragraph (c) of this section and the other provisions of this chapter. (b) (1) The rights of the Government under subsection (a) shall not be exercised by a Federal agency unless it first determines
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a structural flaw that creates a legal contradiction regarding intellectual property rights. The original text clearly assigns ownership of all materials to the owner, while the changed text places a compensation clause before this assignment. This placement implies that the compensation covers the transfer of IP, potentially leading to an unfair assignment of rights if the IP's value significantly exceeds the compensation. 35 U.S. Code § 202 addresses the disposition of rights, even though the focus group isn't federally funded, the principles are relevant. The law snippets state: "Each nonprofit organization or small business firm may, within a reasonable time after disclosure as required by paragraph (c)(1) of this section, elect to retain title to any subject invention". By placing the compensation clause before the assignment of rights, the agreement could be challenged, thus, a contradiction exists.
